meta.title: Web Application Developer | Mateusz Górniak · RubyCon
meta.description: Ruby on Rails developer and fractional CTO in Finland - remote across the EU. Custom web apps, SaaS, legacy migrations, and senior technical leadership for startups and product teams.

hero.label: Web applications · backend
hero.headline: I build web applications your users can rely on.
hero.subline: Custom software from MVP to production. Hands-on development and architecture or fractional CTO support when your team needs a senior lead.

about.label: About
about.title: Developer with a CTO background
about.lead: I build and run web applications, from backend code to technology decisions.
paragraphs: 14+ years building and leading web products. Web developer with experience leading engineering teams as a CTO - I write code and make technology choices that fit the business.
I work with startups, product companies, and product agencies that need senior help without a full-time hire: shipping features, reviewing architecture, or getting through a tough migration.

services.label: Services
services.title: How I can help
services.lead: Web application development and ongoing product work for teams that need a senior developer without a full-time hire.

items.title: Web application development
items.summary: I design and build custom web apps from first release to long-term product work.

points.title: MVPs and new product builds
points.detail: I take you from first prototype to something you can demo, put in users' hands, and keep building on.

points.title: Feature delivery on existing apps
points.detail: I ship new features in your live app - you keep what works today while the product moves forward.

points.title: SaaS and internal tools
points.detail: I build the backend behind your subscription product or internal tool, so it stays reliable as usage grows.

points.title: Integrations with third-party services
points.detail: I connect payments, email, CRM, and other services for you - and keep those integrations working over time.

items.title: Fractional Developer
items.summary: I join your team as a senior developer when you need extra hands in the codebase.

points.title: Backend development and API design
points.detail: I write the core logic and APIs your product runs on - your team can build faster on top.

points.title: Code review and quality improvements
points.detail: I review code with your team - you see fewer surprises in production and cleaner releases over time.

points.title: Mentoring your team
points.detail: I work alongside your developers - you see them grow skills and ship more independently.

points.title: Production debugging
points.detail: I debug live issues with you - you get root-cause fixes, not patches that come back next week.

items.title: Fractional CTO
items.summary: I provide technology leadership without a full-time role.

points.title: Roadmap and architecture
points.detail: I help you set technical direction that matches your business goals - you get a roadmap you can actually follow.

points.title: Team structure and hiring
points.detail: I help you shape the team and hire - you know who to look for and how to organize the work.

points.title: Legacy system assessment
points.detail: I assess your legacy system with you - you get a clear plan for what to keep, replace, or migrate step by step.

points.title: Build vs. buy decisions
points.detail: I walk you through build vs. buy calls - you make decisions you can stand behind later.

stack.label: Stack
stack.title: Technologies I work with
stack.subtitle: For technical readers
stack.lead: Technologies I use most often - chosen for maintainability, not hype. Ruby is where I'm most at home.

primary.name: Ruby
primary.tip: Fast to build and easy to change. A strong fit for MVPs and products that need to evolve quickly.

primary.name: Rails
primary.tip: Ships features with less glue code. Lower maintenance cost and a large pool of developers.

primary.name: PostgreSQL
primary.tip: Reliable data storage that scales with the business. No need to swap databases early.

primary.name: Redis
primary.tip: Keeps the app fast under load. Handles caching and short-lived data without slowing users down.

primary.name: Sidekiq
primary.tip: Background jobs for emails, reports, and imports. The app stays responsive while work runs async.

also.name: TypeScript
also.tip: Catches bugs before release. Safer refactors as the codebase and team grow.

also.name: Node.js
also.tip: Good for APIs and tooling when the team already works in JavaScript.

also.name: Python
also.tip: Useful for integrations, data tasks, and connecting to AI or ML services.

also.name: Docker
also.tip: Same setup in dev and production. Fewer surprises when you deploy.

also.name: AWS
also.tip: Hosting that grows with the product. Turn services on when you need them, not before.

also.name: Stripe
also.tip: Payments, subscriptions, and billing flows that stay reliable as the product grows.

also.name: Elasticsearch
also.tip: Search and filtering that stays fast as your data and user base grow.

also.name: GitHub Actions
also.tip: Automated tests and deploys so releases are repeatable, not nerve-wracking.

also.name: OpenAI
also.tip: Practical AI features in production, with guardrails and review before they reach users.

stack.note: Happy to join your existing stack or help pick the right one for a new project.

situations.title: When to call
items: You need an MVP reliable enough for a demo or early feedback.
The product works, but every release feels stressful.
You want a senior voice on architecture and roadmap - without a full-time CTO.
Legacy makes every new feature expensive or risky to ship.

expertise.title: What I help solve
expertise.lead: Outcome-first areas where I add the most value - from architecture to production reliability.

items.name: System architecture
items.summary: Systems you can extend without rewriting everything from scratch.
items.tip: I design systems from scratch or steer existing ones so you can add features without rewriting, server costs stay reasonable, and scaling is straightforward when traffic grows.

items.name: Team leadership
items.summary: Teams that ship without depending on one person.
items.tip: I help shape the team, agree on how you write and review code, mentor people, and support hiring so shipping does not hinge on one developer.

items.name: Backend development
items.summary: Reliable core logic, databases, and background jobs.
items.tip: I write the core application code: business logic, databases, background jobs. The layer that has to work reliably every day.

items.name: API design
items.summary: Clear interfaces for your product and partners.
items.tip: I design APIs your frontend and partners can build against without surprises as the product grows.

items.name: Monitoring & data
items.summary: Know what happens in production before users complain.
items.tip: I set up monitoring so we are not guessing what happens in production. Bugs surface fast, trends show up in the data, and we track SLOs and spot bottlenecks before users feel them.

items.name: Reliability & production
items.summary: Live systems that stay stable under real traffic.
items.tip: I keep live systems stable: monitoring, incident response, and fewer surprises for users and the business.

items.name: Security & privacy (EU)
items.summary: Sensible security and GDPR-aware choices, proportionate to your product and scale.
items.tip: Practical security for web products: access control, data handling, and privacy choices that fit EU expectations - without slowing delivery.

items.name: Legacy migrations
items.summary: Move forward step by step without stopping the business.
items.tip: I move old codebases forward step by step. The business keeps running, risk goes down, no big-bang rewrite.

items.name: AI-assisted development
items.summary: Faster delivery with AI tools, with human review before production.
items.tip: I use AI where it saves me real time, and I review what goes to production so quality does not slip.

items.question: What is a fractional CTO?
items.answer: Part-time or project-based technology leadership: architecture, roadmap, hiring advice, and senior decisions - without a full-time executive hire.

items.question: Do you only work with Ruby?
items.answer: Ruby on Rails is my main stack and where I deliver fastest. I can join existing stacks or help choose technology for new projects - the goal is maintainable software, not dogma.

items.question: Are you available for remote work from Finland?
items.answer: Yes. I'm based in Finland (Europe/Helsinki) and work remotely with teams spread across Europe. Time zones and async collaboration are normal for me.

items.question: How do we start working together?
items.answer: Send an email with a short description of your situation. We usually start with a call to understand goals and constraints, then agree on scope and next steps.

items.question: What kinds of products do you build?
items.answer: Web applications, SaaS, internal tools, and multi-sided platforms - from MVP to long-running production systems. I focus on backend and architecture; frontends are often handled by your team or partners.

items.question: How do you handle security and privacy?
items.answer: Sensible defaults for access, data, and logging - with GDPR-aware choices where personal data is involved. Proportionate to your product and scale.

howIWork.label: Process
howIWork.title: How I work
howIWork.lead: Business goals first, then the technical plan.

steps.title: Assess
steps.text: Look at the codebase, the team, the constraints, and what success means for the business.

steps.title: Plan
steps.text: Agree on a technical approach with realistic milestones. No unnecessary complexity.

steps.title: Deliver
steps.text: Regular updates, working software, and knowledge passed on to your team.

contact.label: Contact
contact.title: Let's talk
contact.lead: Working on something or want to see if we fit? Drop me a line.

meta.title: Privacy policy | RubyCon
meta.description: How RubyCon handles data on this website: no analytics, theme preference storage, and contact details.

privacy.title: Privacy policy
privacy.rightsBrowserNote: You can clear site data anytime via your browser settings.

sections.title: What this site collects
sections.body: This website does not use analytics or advertising trackers. Fonts are served from this site, not from third-party services. Your light or dark theme choice is stored in your browser (localStorage) so it can be remembered on later visits. If you email me, I receive the information you choose to send.

sections.title: Your rights
sections.body: You can ask for access to or deletion of personal data you have shared with me by email at

sections.title: Changes
sections.body: This notice may be updated occasionally. The latest version is always published on this page.
